key_points: The poem narrates a legend about a woman who was punished for her greed and selfishness.
It teaches a moral lesson about kindness, generosity, and consequences of greed.
The story is set in the Northland and has a folkloric, story-like style.
Themes include morality, consequences of actions, and cultural folklore.
The poem uses simple language and rhyme to make the story engaging for readers.
examples: The old woman hoards food and refuses to share with the needy children.
She is punished by being turned into a woodpecker, symbolizing consequences of greed.
The legend conveys a moral message through a storytelling style.
exam_tips: Focus on the moral of the story: greed leads to punishment.
Remember key characters: the greedy old woman and the children.
Understand the setting and cultural context of the Northland legend.
Pay attention to simple language, rhyme, and storytelling style.
Be able to summarize the story and its moral lesson in your own words.
